body:
### Description

When I run `opencode attach <url>` without `--dir`, attach starts in the server process directory (where `opencode serve` was started), not in the directory where I ran `attach`.

It seems the attach request is missing the client directory context when --dir is omitted, so the server falls back to its own working-directory default.

### Plugins

None

### OpenCode version

1.2.10

### Steps to reproduce

1. In terminal A:
   - `cd /tmp/a`
   - `opencode serve --port 4096`
2. In terminal B:
   - `cd /tmp/b`
   - `opencode attach http://localhost:4096`
3. Attach starts in `/tmp/a` instead of `/tmp/b`.

### Screenshot and/or share link

_No response_

### Operating System

_No response_

### Terminal

_No response_

comments.body:
This issue might be a duplicate of or closely related to existing issues. Please check:
- #4966: Same root cause — `opencode attach` uses server's cwd instead of the client's cwd (closed, but the underlying issue may persist)
- #13732: `attach`: @ file mentions use client-local path instead of remote directory (related path resolution issue with attach)
This sounds like it's working as it's should. If you're attaching, why would you expect opencode to start in a different directory than where it was started as a server?

> This sounds like it's working as it's should. If you're attaching, why would you expect opencode to start in a different directory than where it was started as a server?

Fair, I think this is actually a regression from #8969 though?

#6715 specifically made attach pass the client's `process.cwd()` as the default directory. After #8969 refactored the `--dir` handling, the no-`--dir` path started returning `undefined`, which means no directory header gets sent and the server just falls back to its own cwd.

For context, I run `opencode serve` as a long-lived system service and connect to it from different project directories. In that setup the server's startup directory isn't really useful as a default, the directory I'm running `attach` from is what I'd expect it to use. And `--dir` is already there for when you want to override that.

labels.name: core
labels.description: Anything pertaining to core functionality of the application (opencode server stuff)

title: opencode attach without --dir uses server cwd instead of client cwd
